rules: use context7 when framework, package, sdk, or setup guidance may have changed recently
prefer repository code and docs for local architecture truths
do not rely on model memory alone for rapidly changing external tooling

must: update docs/exec_plans.md before or during substantial work
delegate implementation by ownership boundary when the task spans multiple layers
translate conceptual roles into the correct harness sub-agent type
send completed work through review before presenting it as final
must_not: claim work is complete before required verification passes
treat deprecated modules as active without explicit approval
rewrite frontend UI behavior except for small contract-preserving support changes
change backend persistence or business rules without backend ownership

rules: spawn workers for implementation tasks with clear file ownership
spawn explorers for repo discovery, tracing, and read-only review
use default for reviewer work only when explorer-level review is insufficient
do not assume permanent sub-agent connectivity; assign and spawn per task

backend: validate input with dedicated form requests when introducing or changing request validation
return consistent redirect, flash, validation, or JSON responses that match the existing surface
avoid exposing internal exception details to end users
frontend: surface validation errors from Inertia responses
handle loading, empty, and failure states when behavior changes require them
avoid silent failures in complex UI flows

required_after_code_changes: run focused tests for touched behavior
run npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false after frontend or type changes
required_before_completion: review changed files against this standards document
confirm deprecated modules were not unintentionally re-enabled
summarize any remaining risk or skipped verification

SalesCrm.rule: do not restore, extend, or route new work through this module unless explicitly requested by the Product Owner
